_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Inward turning of the eyelid margin and appendages, usually of the upper eyelid
Corneal / conjunctival damage/ulcer/perforation due to entropion
Typically seen in elderly patients, rarely congenital

preauth.label: Clinical Photograph of the affected eye

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed clinical notes, including history, clinical signs and symptoms, physical examination/investigations and indication, available?
Clinical photo of affected part with proper labelling (R/L) and full face photograph available?

cpd_claim.q: Documents available to show appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice?
OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of surgery?
Documentary evidence present for histopathology examination of specimen?

common_queries: Affected eye not labelled R or L in the clinical photograph for Entropion correction
Diagnosis not backed by clinical signs/symptoms/ophthalmic examination or conservative therapy not attempted/failed for Entropion correction
Evidence of corneal/conjunctival damage or ulcer not documented despite entropion

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Lid Surgeries only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
